traceability_summary: Official police project page plus official EU fact sheet establish public-body involvement, project identity, participant chain, funding line, and pilot framing.
strongest_public_signal: Official police-hosted project page now paired with official CORDIS project metadata and objective text.
strongest_gap: No tender/award/contract identifier; no supplier-normalized buyer-side trace; no operational deployment proof; no public assessment/audit document.
strongest_safe_claim: Public-source project disclosure exists for an AI-assisted border-control initiative and is now supported by an EU-level companion, but procurement and deployment reconstruction remain incomplete.
paper_use_now: Methods/results row for companion-grade project disclosure ceiling and reconstruction-limit figure.

traceability_summary: Direct regulator notice text yields legal basis, remedy, and deployment-context signals.
strongest_public_signal: ICO notice PDF text with Articles 5(1)(a), 6, 9 and Annex 1 remedy language.
strongest_gap: No buyer-side procurement/contract surface or supplier trace in local package.
strongest_safe_claim: The public-sector row supports document-grade governance failure and remedy language, but not acquisition-trace or vendor-trace claims.
paper_use_now: Reviewer-facing regulator-document comparator for disclosure asymmetry.
dashboard_hook: Traceability-ladder panel; legal-basis vs buyer-trace split.

traceability_summary: Direct regulator reprimand text yields DPIA/process-failure and deployment-timing signals.
strongest_public_signal: ICO reprimand PDF text on Article 35(1), March 2023 deployment, and invalid opt-out consent framing.
strongest_gap: No school procurement/supplier trace or public DPIA/impact-assessment document in local package.
strongest_safe_claim: The public-sector row supports document-grade process-failure wording, but not procurement or supplier reconstruction.
dashboard_hook: Traceability-ladder panel; direct-document but no buyer-trace state.

traceability_summary: Public register entry exposes richer governance and supplier metadata than project-page or regulator-only rows.
strongest_public_signal: Named supplier plus human-oversight and impact-assessment signals on a public municipal register surface.
strongest_gap: No public procurement/contract trace, spend identifier, or external validation companion in local package.
strongest_safe_claim: The row is LIMEN's richest current register-style disclosure exemplar, but register visibility alone does not prove procurement or operational efficacy.
paper_use_now: Core register comparator in disclosure-asymmetry figure.

traceability_summary: Register row plus same-buyer procurement package and supplier-consistent award metadata create a stronger middle state than register-only transparency.
strongest_public_signal: Register page plus Hilma award notice 2021-062296 naming Oy IBM Finland Ab with EUR 270992.0.
strongest_gap: Still no exact page-specific proof that the award record is the source contract for the parking-chatbot register row; no explicit legal basis or linked DPIA.
strongest_safe_claim: The Helsinki row now sits above register-only disclosure because buyer-side procurement continuity and supplier-consistent award metadata are public, but the exact row-to-contract linkage remains unproven.
paper_use_now: Middle-state traceability exemplar for methods/results and limitations text.
